events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need output raw JSON. Need assess scenario: field service visit, brakes, observed condition caliper guide pins dry/seized. Work roughed in, not closed. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Caliper guide pins dry/seized means sliding pins lack lubrication and seized; this causes brake binding, uneven pad wear, reduced braking performance, potential safety. Per OEM specs and FMVSS-adjacent shop practice, caliper guide pins must be lubricated with proper brake lubricant, freely moving, functional. Dry/seized is non-compliant/defective installed component. So decision fail. Risk high because brakes impaired, could lead to uneven braking, pull, overheating, loss of control. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 70? rough-complete stage. Need write JSON. confidence 0.95. Findings: guide pins lack lubrication, seized; caliper cannot float; uneven braking/pad wear; non-compliant with OEM. Actions: remove pins, inspect/clean/lubricate with high-temp brake grease, replace boots/pins if corrosion, verify free movement, torque to spec, function test. Rationale: OEM requires caliper guide pins be lubricated and move freely; dry/seized pins compromise brake performance and safety, fail. References: OEM brake service spec, FMVSS No. 135 (hydraulic brake systems) performance, shop brake service standards. Need include order and workable arrays empty since not asked. event_time, rate zero. sound_source empty string. value zero. Need valid JSON only, no markdown.

  "findings": [
    "Caliper guide pins are dry and seized",
    "Caliper cannot slide/float freely, leading to uneven pad application",
    "Condition violates OEM guide-pin lubrication and movement requirements",
    "Potential for brake pull, reduced braking efficiency, overheating, and accelerated pad wear"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove caliper guide pins and inspect for corrosion or damage",
    "Clean pins and bores with proper brake cleaner",
    "Apply manufacturer-approved high-temperature brake lubricant to guide pins",
    "Replace rubber boots and pins if damaged or corroded",
    "Reinstall and torque to OEM specification",
    "Verify caliper slides freely and perform brake function test before closing out"
  ],
  "rationale": "OEM brake service specifications and FMVSS-adjacent shop practice require caliper guide pins to be clean, properly lubricated, and free to move. Dry/seized guide pins prevent the caliper from floating correctly, causing uneven braking, premature wear, and a safety-critical brake performance defect. Because the brake system is installed but not yet closed out and the pins are non-compliant/defective, the work fails inspection.",
  "references": [
    "OEM brake service manual - caliper guide pin lubrication and movement requirements",
    "FMVSS No. 135 / safe brake service practice - brake system performance and maintenance standards"
  ]
}
